summary: Full backend + frontend E2E testing of the Caixa de Mercado (POS) MVP. 12/12 backend pytest cases pass (auth, RBAC, CRUD categories/suppliers/products, stock movements entrada/saida/ajuste, sales with stock decrement & change calc, dashboard reports). Frontend critical flow validated: login -> create product -> PDV add via product card -> checkout (dinheiro, change calc R$40,00) -> Cupom Não Fiscal modal with print/close -> sale persisted in /vendas (2 rows). One critical UX bug: barcode scan via Enter key does not add product to cart (subtotal stays R$0,00) even though backend GET /products/barcode works.

ui_bugs.issue: Pressing Enter on barcode input does NOT add the product to the cart. Reproduced twice with a valid barcode (E2E1779084346). Form's onSubmit either does not fire or addToCart() state update is lost. Clicking the product card works fine. This breaks the primary 'bipe e venda' workflow.

action_items: Fix POS barcode Enter-to-scan: verify handleScan e.preventDefault + api.get('/products/barcode/{code}') + addToCart triggers cart update. Possibly setScanInput("") is happening before addToCart causing stale state, or the form is not wrapping the input correctly after rerender. Add a console.log or check that onSubmit fires on Enter.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 552 lines - acceptable for MVP but consider splitting into routers (auth, products, sales, reports) before features grow.

Sales endpoint decrements stock AFTER inserting the sale doc and is not transactional — a partial failure between sale insert and stock update could leave stock inconsistent. Consider Motor transactions or compensating logic.
Stock validation in /sales loops products sequentially; concurrent sales of same product could oversell (race condition). Acceptable for MVP single-cashier but document risk.
ProductIn uses float for price/stock — risk of floating-point rounding errors; consider Decimal or store as integer cents.
CORS allow_credentials=True with allow_origins='*' is silently invalid per spec; set explicit origins via CORS_ORIGINS env.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (1 critical UX bug on barcode Enter scan; receipt + sale flow OK)

test_credentials: admin@mercado.com / admin123 ; operador@mercado.com / operador123 (both verified working)
seed_data_creation: Tests create TEST_ prefixed categories/suppliers/products and clean up via DELETE. One E2E product (barcode E2E*) was created via UI and left in DB.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is solid (12/12 pytest). Re-run frontend flow after main agent fixes barcode Enter-to-cart bug in /app/frontend/src/pages/POS.jsx (handleScan). Sidebar nav-* data-testids exist in code (Layout.jsx) but querySelectorAll for them returned partial results in one script run and 0 in another — likely timing; not a real bug. Reports/Estoque/Vendas pages all load and render data.
rca of the issue: Barcode Enter scan: handleScan is bound to <form onSubmit>. Pressing Enter while focused on pos-barcode-input should submit, call GET /api/products/barcode/{code}, then addToCart(data). Backend endpoint works (verified by pytest). Suspect either: (1) the form has no submit button so Enter does not submit in some browsers — but React's onSubmit on form usually catches it; (2) setScanInput('') in handleScan runs before addToCart promise resolves, but addToCart uses returned data not state; (3) handleScan may throw before reaching addToCart if response shape differs. Add try/catch logging and verify e.preventDefault() is called and addToCart is invoked.
